data.title: EverCommerce's Growth Slows As Cost Cutting Progress Wanes
data.description:
Investment Outlook

EverCommerce Inc. (NASDAQ:EVCM) provides a range of business software to small and medium businesses.

I previously wrote about EVCM in April 2024 with a Hold outlook due to slower growth amid long-term transformation efforts.

The firm's revenue growth rate continues to fall, progress on cost-cutting has stalled, and the U.S. consumer economic outlook is negative, so I'm bearish (sell) on EVCM in the near term.

EverCommerce's Market And Approach

Per a 2023 market research report by MarketsandMarkets, the worldwide market for field service management software was estimated at $4 billion in 2023 and is expected to exceed $7.3 billion by 2028.

If achieved, this would represent a CAGR of a fairly impressive 12.8% from 2024 to 2028.

This growth is expected to be driven by improving technologies due to increasing customer demands and the need for increased efficiencies.

Also, the promise of generative AI enhancements to systems to improve field decision-making and other optimization strategies will compel vendors to enhance their offerings.

The chart below illustrates the growth differential by major region, with the Asia Pacific region expected to grow at the fastest rate through 2028:

Major competitive industry players include the following companies:

  • PTC (ServiceMax).

  • Salesforce.

  • Intuit.

  • Block.

  • HubSpot.

  • Manual processes.

  • Numerous point solution competitors.

EverCommerce focuses on specific verticals such as home and field services, health service providers and fitness and wellness service providers.

The company markets its software solutions through a typical "land and expand" approach that's common for subscription-based software companies that aim to start in one area or segment and expand into other segments as the company proves itself.

77.2% of EVCM's total revenue came from subscriptions and transaction fees for the Q2 2024 quarter, as the pie chart shows here:

Recent Financial Trends

Total revenue by quarter (columns) has risen only modestly year-over-year due to a slowing growth rate. Operating income by quarter (red line) has remained flat because of moderating revenue growth and flat expenses.

The degree of operating leverage has been highly variable, with lower lows in recent quarters.

Gross profit margin by quarter (blue line) has varied within a very narrow range. Selling and G&A expenses as a percentage of total revenue by quarter (orange line) have dropped due to cost control efforts by management, while R&D expenses as a function of revenue (purple line) have remained essentially flat.

Earnings per share (Diluted) have varied widely in recent quarters, although making headway again toward breakeven in Q2 2024.

(All data in the above charts is GAAP).

For balance sheet results, EVCM ended the quarter with $86.7 million in cash and equivalents and $531 million in total debt, of which only $5.5 million was short-term and the rest long-term.

Over the trailing twelve months, free cash flow was $97.8 million and capital expenditures were only $2.9 million.

The company paid $23.8 million in stock-based compensation in the last four quarters, so the free cash flow net of SBC was $74.0 million.

The net dollar decrease in stock outstanding for the last four quarters was valued at $55.5 million, so the company has repurchased stock at a significant rate.

In the past 12 months, EVCM's stock price has gained only 3.8% vs. that of the benchmark iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF's (IGV) rise of 34%, as the chart indicates below.

I've included a handy major metrics table for reference below:

Metric

Amount

EV/Sales - FWD

3.5

EV/EBITDA - FWD

14.1

Rev. Growth Estimate - FWD

5.5%

2024 EPS Estimate - FWD

$0.49

Price/Sales

2.8

Revenue Growth - TTM

6.2%

Net Income Margin

-6.3%

EBITDA Margin

16.3%

Market Capitalization

$1,930,000,000

Enterprise Value

$2,400,000,000

Operating Cash Flow

$100,680,000

EPS (Fully Diluted)

-$0.23

Free Cash Flow/Share

$0.42

Seeking Alpha Quant Score

Hold - 2.72

(Source: Seeking Alpha).

EVCM's Rule of 40 performance has worsened in the past few quarters from the standpoint of slowing topline revenue outpacing slightly higher operating margin, as the table indicates here:

Rule of 40 Performance (Unadjusted)

Q4 2023

Q2 2024

Revenue Growth %

8.8%

6.2%

Operating Margin

3.5%

4.1%

Total

12.3%

10.3%

(Source: Seeking Alpha).

Why I'm Bearish On EverCommerce

EverCommerce's revenue growth rate continues to slow while the firm isn't making much progress on operating profitability.

A brighter spot has been the company's payments revenue stream, which grew 8% year-over-year, faster than its overall topline revenue growth.

While management said its efficiency efforts have made progress, we won't see more significant results until 2025, but those may add to operating profitability.

I've prepared a recent conference call sentiment indicator graphic:

The chart illustrates that the firm's marketing technology solutions segment continues to face market headwinds while "macro" trends continue to be stable, so leadership has left full-year 2024 guidance unchanged.

Forward revenue growth rate is expected to slow to 5.5% from 6.2%, a negative trend.

Regarding valuation, the market is valuing EVCM at an EV/sales multiple of around 3.5x on an estimated NTM revenue growth rate of only 5.5% against a median Meritech Capital SaaS Index implied ARR growth rate of around 17% (Source).

Also, the benchmark index indicates a median Net Dollar Retention Rate of 110% as of October 2024, while EVCM's core net revenue retention rate was only 97%, substantially worse than the benchmark.

The index of publicly held SaaS application software companies showed a forward EV/revenue multiple median of around 5.8x on Oct. 4, 2024, as the chart shows here:

So, by comparison, EVCM is currently rightly valued by the market at a significant multiple discount to the broader index.

While management intends to continue to invest in its EverHealth and EverPro home and field service segments, gains in these efforts will need to more than offset headwinds in its marketing technology solutions segment.

The U.S. consumer continues to weaken as the pandemic-era stimulus wanes and spending has become more cautious.

The most recent consumer confidence survey by The Conference Board showed a sharp fall from 105.6 in August to 98.7 in September.

The present situation index also fell by 10.3 points to 124.3. A chart of the various figures is shown here:

According to the survey release, "Consumer confidence dropped in September to near the bottom of the narrow range that has prevailed over the past two years," said Dana M. Peterson, Chief Economist at The Conference Board.

"September's decline was the largest since August 2021 and all five components of the Index deteriorated. Consumers' assessments of current business conditions turned negative while views of the current labor market situation softened further. Consumers were also more pessimistic about future labor market conditions and less positive about future business conditions and future income."

With an apparently softening economic environment in the U.S., which is 70% driven by consumer spending, falling forward revenue growth rate and stale progress on further cost-cutting, my outlook on EVCM's near-term prospects is to sell.

data.title: Alibaba: China's Stimulus Effect Is Already Wearing Off
data.description:
Since I last wrote about China’s e-commerce giant Alibaba (NYSE:BABA) in August, its price is up by an impressive 20%. I didn't see any catalyst for its rise at the time, going by its underwhelming results, even as its market multiples looked alright.

But a catalyst did appear. In the form of a monetary stimulus to the Chinese economy. A spate of monetary easing measures by the country's central bank, People's Bank of China (PBoC) in late September, resulted in a rally in China-focused stocks. The Shanghai Composite (SHCOMP), which was going nowhere until then in 2024, is up by more than 20% since PBoC's measures and more than 10% year to date. The anticipation of a fiscal stimulus also supports them further, though its extent is only speculative right now.

The question for Alibaba, though, is whether the macroeconomic story is enough to sustain its price rally. Here I argue that it isn't and that signs of a come-off are already visible.

China's economy still looks lackluster...

The stimulus measures could impact China's growth positively. But only a bit. Goldman Sachs raised its forecast for 2024 from 4.7% to 4.9%. And from 4.3% to 4.7% for 2025. However, this is little solace for the following reasons:

  • With growth at 5% in H1 2024, the GDP is actually expected to see a slightly slower increase in H2 2024 even now.
  • Even with the forecast upgrade, the figures for both 2024 and 2025 are still lower. China's trend growth is at 5.9%, where trend growth is defined as the average growth of the economy in the last 10 years. Further, the gap between the forecasts and pre-COVID-19 trend growth is even higher, at 7.7%.
  • Not all forecasters are positive. In its just released World Economic Outlook, the IMF slightly reduced China's growth outlook to 4.8% from 5% earlier. It also maintained its forecast for 2025 at 4.5%. The fund further points out that "Deeper- or longer-than-expected contraction in China’s property sector, especially if it leads to financial instability, could weaken consumer sentiment..."

... which reflects in retail sales

For Alibaba, the macroeconomic context is important given its massive domestic e-commerce segment, the Taobao and Tmall Group [TTG]. The division brought in 46% of the company's total revenues in Q1 FY25, and its net income was higher than the company's total net income due to losses in other divisions. But support from the segment is limited, with a 1% YoY revenue contraction during the quarter.

China's online retail sales figures available for up to September don't show improvements either. As the chart below shows, cumulative online sales growth has broadly been winding down since February and up to August. There's some hope though, as official data for the first nine months of the year sees some improvement to 8.6% YoY, compared to 8.1% up to August. More recently, the company has reported strong demand from its Single's Day campaign, but how far these developments translate into quarterly figures remains to be seen.

Doesn't compare well with peers

Based on relatively underwhelming macro trends and forecasts, it's hard to see any upside to Alibaba's financials for now. But the price gains on news of the stimulus have raised the trailing twelve months [TTM] GAAP price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio to 26.4x.

It might have come off from 30.9x in early October, but it's still higher than the 21.9x level at the time I last checked. Moreover, it's still close to the highest levels in the past year (see chart below).

Importantly, at this level, the ratio is unattractive compared to BABA's peers like Temu owner PDD (PDD) and JD.com (JD), which are trading at 13.9x and 14.8x, respectively. If Alibaba's financials were commensurately superior, the premium could still be justified. But that's not the case either.

During the last TTM, Alibaba's revenue growth at 5.9% is nowhere close to the 106.7% increase for PDD, even though it's slightly ahead of JD, which grew by 3.3%. To be fair, PDD's revenues are still less than half those of Alibaba's, which explains part of the growth. But the fact remains - PDD's diluted EPS growth of 137.7% over this time is also far ahead of Alibaba, which has actually seen a 14.7% decline. In fact, even JD has seen much better EPS performance than Alibaba, with a very healthy 45% rise in EPS.

There's something to be said for Alibaba's promising cloud services segment's potential to contribute to profits. After seeing a 49% rise in the last financial year, the segment saw a 155% YoY rise in EBITA in the latest quarter. But as I pointed out the last time, it's still too soon to bet solely on it right now. For context, in the past quarter it contributed to less than 11% of the company's revenues and just 5.2% to the adjusted EBITA. In the meantime, the gap between BABA's and its peers P/Es is still rather big, especially considering the present financials.

What next?

With this as the backdrop, it's little wonder that BABA has already come off from the highs of early October by almost 17%. Sure, this could be a reflection of profit taking after the stock reached its highest levels in a year, but it also shows that there isn't sustained optimism on its prospects either.

China's stock markets might have received support from a monetary stimulus, but going by forecasts, that's unlikely to stop macros from fizzling out for the remainder of 2024 and into 2025. This can continue to show up in TTG's underwhelming performance, which doesn't sit well with the stock's high P/E and unconvincing comparison with peers. At any other time, BABA would be a Sell. But considering that its forward non-GAAP P/E at 11.4x is still lower than the five-year average of 16.6x, I'm retaining a Hold rating.

Editor's Note: This article discusses one or more securities that do not trade on a major U.S. exchange. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.

data.title: Onto Innovation Surges With AI Tailwinds, Yet Steep Valuation Suggest Overreach
data.description:
Onto Innovation Inc. (NYSE:ONTO) specializes in process control tools in the semiconductor manufacturing space. Specifically, it holds deep expertise in optical metrology and chip packaging, both of which are extremely crucial, especially in light of the current demand surge semiconductor players are experiencing with the AI proliferation. While the company's recent financial performance is beyond impressive, I do not see its current price as being fair, in light of how it compares to many of its peers. For these reason, I rate the stock as a sell.

Overview

Precision and quality are critical in the semiconductor manufacturing industry, throughout every step of the product development chain. Given the deep design complexity involved, producers of semiconductor wafers and integrated chips typically deal with very low levels of defect tolerance, making specialized process control tools critical.

This is where Onto Innovation Inc. (ONTO) comes into the picture, specializing primarily in metrology and inspection tools. Through its advanced optical metrology offers, semiconductor IC fabricators and wafer producers are able to meet precise quality standards, and adequately carry out wafer macro-defect inspection. Moreover, Onto is positioned to cater to both the early stages of silicon wafer production ("front-end" manufacturing) and the later stages of chip packaging and testing ("back-end" manufacturing), giving it broad exposure in this space. As I shall elaborate below, ONTO is benefitting from a boom in demand of AI packaging and metrology systems. I believe the market is still in an early phase of adoption for these systems.

Performance Assessment

The company came in with strong topline growth this quarter, with its net revenue surging by over 27% from $191 million $242 million. The company's systems and software segment in particular, which makes up 87% of its total sales, saw a YoY climb of 32%, during the quarter. This is encouraging, considering that in the full results of 2023, this segment had declined by 21%. The boom is driven primarily by the company's Dragonfly G3 system, a defect inspection tool that semiconductor packaging players utilize to maximize yields. In particular, the surge in the product's demand seems linked to increased activity surround AI and high-bandwidth memory packaging. In fact, booming AI demand has brought the company its fourth consecutive quarterly revenue record. Throughout these reporting periods, AI-related packaging accounted for more than half of the company's total revenue, as revealed by CEO Michael Plisinski, in the latest earnings call.

The broader AI trend causing a boom in advanced packaging makes sense, considering traditional chip designs are fast becoming outdated to handle low latency and high-speed data transfer requirements for AI applications. This trend is likely to continue because, as Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang pointed out, AI demand is likely to continue surpassing available supply, well into 2025. With these supply/demand conditions, existing players such as Onto Innovation, which do hold deep expertise in critical AI chip process system tools like packaging, are likely to enjoy a strong pricing environment. With these strong trends, as it is reflective by strong sales in the Dragonfly platform, the company projects the inspection side of its business to take on a climb exceeding 70% by the end of the year.

I am also pleased to see that ONTO's operating income during the quarter, almost doubling from Q2 last year, in a YoY jump from $26 million to $50.2 million. Its recent operating income figure also happens to be the highest reported in the last 6 quarters. On the margin side, things seem even more impressive, with an expansion of over 700 basis points from 13.64% to 20.70%. Although R&D during Q2 of both 2023 and 2024 have been the same, at $27 million, the larger revenue this year has resulted in a far more expanded operating income. This is a positive, as R&D investments seem to be paying off, especially in terms of achieving further tech differentiation and extending product capabilities. These efforts are paving the way for Onto to expand its future hybrid bonding metrology and inspection market, along with 3D metrology for server markets.

Also, the operating cash flows in Q2 were strong at $65.3 million, which means Onto's business model is solid, and capable of converting 100% of its operating income into cash flow. A large part of the success on this front is attributable to strong inventory management, despite dealing with surging demand. On the bottom line, the net income doubled from $29 million to $53 million, boosting the margin from 13.58% to 21.85%. This amounts to an 827 bp expansion, and is boosted by investment income this quarter, amounting to $8.5 million. Finally, I would like to add that Onto has a very healthy balance sheet, with zero debt. Normally, when a company is trying to meet booming demand, it takes on substantial debt in order to expand its supply. I find it promising that this is not the case with Onto:

Additionally, the company's total liabilities amount to less than $175 million, which are dwarfed in comparison to its total assets, which exceed $2 billion.

Overall, I believe ONTO is doing very well financially, and is well positioned to see its topline performance continue to soar along the boom in demand for AI packaging by semiconductor companies.

Valuation

In order to gauge how fairly valued Onto Innovation actually is, I have selected a peer group of companies that specialize in the process control domain of the semiconductor manufacturing industry, and more specifically those that engage in metrology systems. For this, I have chosen the following peers, alongside ONTO:

After selecting these companies for a comparison on multiples, I realized that Onto's valuation metrics point to it being priced the highest compared to all of the selected peers:

The multiples above indicate that ONTO shows a pricing that is a lot higher than most of its peers, in terms of its earnings, both trailing and forward, as well as the revenue it is generating, both trailing and forward. Its enterprise value for every dollar of EBITDA earned is also far higher than its peers, which points to an overpricing.

Normally, when a stock is priced higher than its peers so consistently, it is because of some premium the market is assigning to it, due to a higher level of growth. However, this does not appear to be the case with ONTO, as shown by its forward EPS (diluted) growth rate. It also seems that the company has not been as effective as its peers in building up its assets and fortifying its balance sheet in the last three years:

Overall, looking at these metrics holistically, it is apparent that ONTO's current price of $206 seems much too high given its top and bottom-line performance, and its growth rate, in a relative sense. Its current performance does show that it is enjoying strong growth, as a result of booming AI packaging demand, but its growth prospects are still not as impressive as those demonstrated by its peers.

For these reasons, I rate the stock as a sell.

Risk

While the numbers clearly show that, despite its performance gains, ONTO is priced consistently higher than its peers, despite lagging behind in terms of growth, I believe there is one risk to my sell thesis. ONTO is currently enjoying some strong upward momentum, despite what its price multiples show. Below, we can see that, compared to its peers, ONTO stock has surged the highest in terms of its price, in the last six months:

I know that the growth metrics and price multiples do not support this rise, but for some reason (which I believe may be linked to sentiment), the market is driving up the price of ONTO. Selling the stock now, which my thesis suggests, could deprive shareholders of additional gains. In fact, those who buy high here could potentially sell even higher, but I would not recommend such an approach due to the risks it holds.

Among its peers, ONTO is the only stock with a positive SMA200 of 3%, and the lowest negative SMA50 indicator, highlighting that it fares well against its peers in terms of momentum:

While the momentum is promising, I do not think chasing temporary highs is worth the risk of a swift correction. I maintain my sell rating on the stock.

Investment Takeaway

Onto Innovation, with its deep expertise in the semiconductor process control space, especially through metrology systems and advanced packaging, is seeing strong financial gains, amid the broader AI demand boom. The growth in its topline, operating income, and bottom line are impressive on their own, but so are the company's expanding profit margins. While this is all impressive, I believe the stock's price is priced at too much of a premium that seems justified. This is especially so when we see how much ONTO lags behinds its peers in crucial, forward-looking growth metrics. For these reasons, I rate the stock as a sell.

data.title: aTyr Pharma: The Bull Has Finally Come Out
data.description:
Introduction

Biotech company aTyr Pharma (NASDAQ:ATYR) has a special year coming up, with two readouts, including a very pivotal one in pulmonary sarcoidosis, scheduled for the third quarter of 2025. The stock has been performing tremendously well lately, having gained 136% over the past year, with the most gains since the start of October 2024.

The company has one drug in development, Efzofitimod, which is supposed to modulate inflammation through NRP2 without immune suppression. The lack of immunosuppressive activity would set the drug candidate apart from more traditional inhibitors of inflammation, namely TNF inhibitors. The Phase 1b/2 readout in pulmonary sarcoidosis was not all too promising as it had failed to reach statistical significance, but this was a very small study that probably was not powered for that. The company also established that the lowest dose was subtherapeutic. A post-hoc study yielded interesting results in the higher doses, on the basis of which the company believes that it can go for a successful Phase 3 trial. As for SCC-ILD, the ongoing Phase 2 study is a small study which may fail to reach statistical significance as well. However, Atyr seems to have learned to design its trial better in light of earlier results. Data from this trial should be reported in Q2 2025.

The company has $82 million in cash at the end of Q2 2024, which should have sufficient financing to fund operations until the upcoming readouts. The stock has been breaking out lately. Although I do not believe this is an almost-certain win, if the company succeeds in its Phase 3 trial in pulmonary sarcoidosis, a huge market could open up. No new drug has been approved for pulmonary sarcoidosis for the past 60 years. That warrants a buy thesis.

There is little competition in the space. Novartis (NVS) results from CMK-389 have just been reported, showing a -1.49% difference in pulmonary function with placebo, and more patients on drug needing higher steroid dosages during the trial. Roivant (ROIV) should report on its Phase 2 trial with namilumab soon.

Atyr Pharma

aTyr Pharma is testing efzofitimod in a Phase 3 trial in pulmonary sarcoidosis and in a Phase 2 trial in SSC-ILD. Efzofitimod should resolve inflammation by modulating NRP2 and thereby prevent fibrosis, without immunosuppression. NRP2 is a target in the inflammatory pathway which is unregulated in granulomas of patients suffering from sarcoidosis, and skin lesion of patients with systemic sclerosis.

Phase 1b/2 trial in pulmonary sarcoidosis

The company had previously run a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study that tested efzofitimod in 37 patients with pulmonary sarcoidosis over the course of 24-weeks. Patients had to taper steroid use in the first 8 weeks of the study.

Efzofitimod was found to be safe and well-tolerated, but results were not statistically significant. However, a post hoc analysis had concluded that:

- disease control was maintained with decreased or discontinued steroid use;

- time-to-first-relapse was 126 days in the subtherapeutic group (1.0 mg/kg efzofitimod and placebo) and only 1 patient in the therapeutic group had relapsed by the end of the study (3.0 and 5.0 mg/kg efzofitimod);

- more than half of patients in the subtherapeutic group relapsed following steroid taper, compared to less than 10% of patients in the therapeutic group.

- pulmonary functions as measured by forced vital capacity or FVC improved in a dose-dependent manner, with the 5 mg/kg group ending up at about 2 points difference from baseline:

- symptoms related to dyspnea, cough, fatigue, and lung and general health, improved in a dose-dependent manner compared to placebo.

This is the slide that the company includes on efficacy in its corporate presentation.

These are interesting analyses, but one should not forget that they are post-hoc, as Seeking Alpha analyst Stephen Ayers had pointed out. Toleration of steroid tapering could be easier in patients with higher FVC levels, which could lead to investigator bias. Also, in light of baseline differences, perhaps one can only conclude that patients with mild disease had better outcomes than those with moderate disease, regardless of the group (placebo or drug).

Phase 2 trial in systemic sclerosis

Systemic sclerosis is an inflammatory condition characterized by fibrosis in connective tissues such as the skin. SSC-ILD affects the lungs primarily and affects about 100,000 people in the US.

aTyr’s Phase 2 trial is a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study in 25 patients with SSC-ILD over the course of 28 weeks.

This is the trial design, which now includes an open label access program.

Preliminary data from this trial should be reported in Q2 2025. It is not clear yet what exactly the company will report on. If efzofitimod is able to show improvement of skin thickness, then that may indicate disease-modification contrary to steroidal drugs.

Phase 3 trial in pulmonary sarcoidosis

Sarcoidosis is an inflammatory condition characterized by lesions in one or more organs, the origin of which is unknown. Pulmonary sarcoidosis affects 90% of all patients. The incidence of the disease is about 200,000 patients in America and 1.2 million worldwide. The only standard of care for patients at this time is steroidal and immunosuppressive. In the long run, these treatments cause toxicity issues and efficacy remains elusive.

The current standard of care for pulmonary sarcoidosis is also mainly steroids, and additionally immunosuppressants and anti-TNF antibodies.

This is the trial design of aTyr’s Phase 3 trial in 268 patients with pulmonary sarcoidosis over 52 weeks.

The trial’s primary endpoint is steroid burden, to be reported as a function of its tapering from baseline over the course of a year. Key secondary endpoints will be lung function and symptom control.

The good thing about aTyr’s Phase 3 trial design is that the company seems to have learned from its previous trial, by doubling the trial period, enrolling about 7 times as many patients, and focusing on higher dosages. If that would pay off, then the trial is set for success. Part of the secret sauce of this trial could be in the details. The requirements on steroid tapering appear to be more strict compared to the Phase 1b/2 trial, which may be advantageous insofar as patients on placebo may more easily relapse. Whereas the Phase 2 required steroid tapering, the Phase 3 requires it to be considered from day 15 and that patients should be off steroids by week 16, with successive attempts to stop steroid use in the following weeks. The Phase 2 only required tapering to 5mg by week 8, and only responders to therapy needed to be off steroids by week 16. As a result, relapse rates in the untreated populations may be even higher compared to the post-hoc results seen from the Phase 1b/2 trial.

The study has received two positive data safety monitoring board reviews.

On August 13, 2024, aTyr announced full enrollment of its Phase 3, which should report data in the third quarter of 2025. If that trial is successful, I believe aTyr could aim for a BLA-filing by mid-2026, approval about six months thereafter, and a subsequent drug launch some time by 2026-2027.

Valuation

At the time of writing, Atyr has a $250 million market cap. The market for pulmonary sarcoidosis, for which no drug has been approved in the past 60 years, is expected to reach $53.3 in value by 2034. The Phase 3 trial for Efzofitimod tries to replace first-line therapy, so that value could be unlocked in case of success. There are about 200,000 patients in the US and more than 1 million worldwide.

I am assuming a $25,000 cost of therapy for intravenous administration of probably the highest dose of 5mg/kg, which had shown the best efficacy before. That assumption is probably fairly moderate as the median price of a new drug in 2022 was $222,000, although I do not believe this can be asked for the treatment of pulmonary sarcoidosis. I'm assuming about 30% of total eligible patients can be on the drug at peak sales, which would lead to annual peak sales of about $1.5 billion. Even a fraction of that could lead to a valuation very different from the current one.

I'm assuming a high-risk scenario given earlier failures of anti-inflammatory drugs, at 50%, with drug approval possible in 2027, and a 5x revenue multiple. The projected valuation would then be about $2.22 billion, which is about ten times the current market valuation. If one would account for a higher market price, higher market penetration or a lower discount, these numbers could even be drastically higher.

Wall Street analysts have an average price target of $21 on the stock, which represents more than 500% upside.

The additional SSC-ILD market could open up an additional venue of revenue in the longer term, in case of success.

Finances

As of June 30, 2024, aTyr had $81.4 million in cash and cash equivalents. The company had picked up financing both in 2023 and in 2024. The company still has an ATM facility in place allowing it to provide further financing.

Last year’s cash burn was $58 million. That should allow the company to report data on both trials without needing additional further financing, though I assume the ATM facility will still be used in the months to come. That ATM has been entered into in 2022, and has led to net proceeds of $18.4 million in 2023, and $21.3 million for the six months ending June 30,2024 (10-Q, page 15). That means $25.3 of that ATM remains to be used.

In case of success in the Phase 3 trial, the company may need to pick up additional financing for the purposes of commercialization. To that purpose, I assume, there is an S-3 filing of November 9, 2023 for up to $300 million in common stock, preferred stock, debt securities and warrants.

Ownership

Recent insider buying activity would suggest bullish sentiment. Insiders have made several purchases over the past year. This includes three purchases for about $185,000 by non-executive director Paul Schimmel. CEO Sanjay Shukla has been a steady buyer over the past years. That said, at 2.39%, insider ownership is low.

However, institutional ownership is fairly high for a biotech of this size, at 63% of all shares.

Risks

aTyr Pharma is an easy-to-understand company because it only has one drug candidate in its pipeline, namely efzofitimod. That also means that, if that drug candidate fails to deliver, such failure may impact the entire company pipeline.

The Phase 1b/2 study in pulmonary sarcoidosis was a small study. Little is known about the exact causes of pulmonary sarcoidosis. Hence, the disease may be heterogenous and the post-hoc efficacy seen in the Phase 1b/2 trial may not be able to be repeated in a Phase 3 trial.

Little is known about long-term efficacy of NRP2 modulators. NRP2 is stated to be a target upstream the inflammatory pathway, but it is possible that the body finds other ways to transmit the inflammatory signal, leading to relapse over a longer period of time.

Efzofitimod is stated not to be immunosuppressive, but how exactly that’s the case is unclear. I contrast this with the clarity of a drug candidate like INmune Bio’s (INMB) Xpro, making the same claim for the CNS related to the selective inhibition of pro-inflammatory soluble TNF.

Also, other anti-inflammatory agents have been tried in pulmonary sarcoidosis, but have failed, such as infliximab, ustekinumab, golimumab and adalimumab, though some of these trials did see an efficacy signal. Infliximab showed improvement in FVC values, and adalimumab showed some pulmonary function efficacy in 80% of patients. Golimumab and Ustekinumab did not show efficacy.

There is, of course, always competitive risk. One particular company with an earlier topline Phase 2 readout on 100 patients with pulmonary sarcoidosis is Roivant Sciences (ROIV), with its anti-GM-CSF monoclonal antibody namilumab. My coverage of that company can be found here. Roivant is only targeting sarcoidosis with namilumab.

Further competitors are Molecure, Xentria and Relief Therapeutics. In March 2024, Molecure initiated a Phase 2 trial with a CHIT-1 antibody, which is also being tested in MASH, udiopathic pulmonary fibrosis and interstitial lung disease. After a successful Phase 1 trial showing minimal side effects, Xentria is running a Phase 2 trial with XTMAB-16, an anti-TNF antibody. Though Relief Therapeutics (OTCQB:RLFTY) has received Orphan Drug Designation for the treatment of sarcoidosis in 2021, its pipeline does not feature sarcoidosis yet. Novartis (NVS) ran a Phase 2 trial with an IL-18 targeting antibody called CMK-389. Results on the latter has recently been filled out in ClinicalTrials.gov, showing a -1.49 treatment difference between drug and placebo on FVC, with a p-value of 0.1804 meaning results are not statistically significant. Additionally, more patients on drug needed higher steroid dosages during the trial than patients on placebo. An almost equal amount of patients deteriorated during the trial (31% on drug versus 32.3% on placebo).

Conclusion

Biotech company aTyr Pharma has an exciting year ahead, with a Phase 3 trial in pulmonary sarcoidosis and a Phase 2 trial in SSC-ILD to report on.

The Phase 3 trial in pulmonary sarcoidosis is particularly interesting, as the market that could open up in case of success could be enormous. The setup of that Phase 3 trial is good. Although aTyr's Phase 1b/2 trial had failed to show efficacy, a post-hoc analysis had shown that the therapeutic dosages did prevent patients from relapsing on steroids, and allowed disease control. The Phase 3 trial works with these dosages, and could therefore confirm what was seen in the post-hoc analysis in a statistically significant manner. The Phase 2 trial in SSC-ILD is probably of less importance for investors as it only intends to enroll 25 patients, and may therefore not be able to report statistical significance. However, it may also allow insight into therapeutic doses and efficacy.

My valuation exercise leads me to a fair valuation of about $2 billion at this time. With $81 million in the bank and an ATM facility, Atyr is well-funded for operations over the course of the coming year. For the above reasons, I am giving aTyr a Buy rating at this stage.
